data.title: Goldman's top U.S. stock strategist David Kostin will retire this year - report
data.description:
David Kostin, the chief U.S. equity strategist at Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), will retire at the end of the year after joining the investment bank in 1994, according to a Thursday media report.

The Wall Street analyst will become an advisory director at Goldman (NYSE:GS), Bloomberg reported, citing a memo, which added that Ben Snider will succeed him. 

Snider joined the company in 2010 as an analyst, followed by his appointment to managing director in 2017.

The article noted that a Goldman (GS) spokesperson confirmed the memo.

Last week, Kostin projected the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates a total of three times this year, aligning with the central bank's median projection.

He recommended asset managers, companies with high floating rate debt and gold miners as promising investments in the run-up to the last quarter of the year.

data.title: CDC vaccines panel head Kulldorff challenges former directors to debate
data.description:
Martin Kulldorff, the chair of the CDC's influential Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices, said that for the public to know whom to trust on vaccine advice, former CDC directors should come forward for "a live public debate on vaccines."

Kulldorff, speaking at a two-day meeting running through Friday where recommendations on the measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR), hepatitis B, and COVID-19 vaccines will be discussed and voted on, said "If they are unwilling to engage in an open and honest debate with the chair of a committee that they are so severely criticizing, then I advise that you should not trust them."

Kulldorff, along with the other 11 members of the committee, are all new to the panel. Seven of them were assigned in June in the wake of HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. dimissing all the previous members. Another five were added a few days ago. 

"We are currently experiencing heated controversies about vaccines, and a key question is: Who can you trust?" Kulldorff said at the meeting. "Here is my advice. When there are different scientific views, only trust scientists who are willing to engage with and publicly debate the scientists with other views."

He also refuted the idea that the panel is against vaccines. "The members of this ACIP committee are committed to reassuring the public and restoring public confidence by removing unnecessary risks and harms whenever possible. That is a pro-vaccine agenda."

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nvidia, Intel sign blockbuster deal to co-develop chips; Nvidia to take $5B stake in Intel
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Nvidia shares rose 3.5% in late morning trading, while Intel soared 28%, hitting their highest in over a year. 

Tech Stock Pros, Investing Group Leader for Tech Contrarians, said the deal is a “tactical move” from Nvidia, especially as it relates to AMD (AMD) and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM).

“We suspect Nvidia is trying to appease the Trump administration, which already has a stake in Intel, in return for potentially getting approval on the B30A sale to China and an overall smoother negotiation with [Chinese President] Xi on tomorrow’s call,” they said via email. “The deal carries far greater weight for Intel, as it could help revive the company’s competitive edge against AMD and potentially lead to higher pricing power and market share.”

AMD shares slid 2.7% in late morning trading, while Taiwan Semi — which manufactures chips for Nvidia, AMD, Apple (AAPL) and others — rose 1.7%.

As part of the deal, the two companies will use Nvidia's NVLink, bringing Nvidia's artificial intelligence and accelerated computing strength and Intel's x86 architecture. Intel will build Nvidia-custom x86 CPUs for the data center, and Intel will build and offer x86 system-on-chips that integrate into Nvidia's RTX GPUs. The RTX system-on-chips will be aimed at the PC market.

In addition to the deal, Nvidia said it would invest $5B in Intel's common stock at a price of $23.28 per share. The investment is subject to customary closing conditions, including required regulatory approvals, the companies said.

“AI is powering a new industrial revolution and reinventing every layer of the computing stack — from silicon to systems to software. At the heart of this reinvention is NVIDIA’s CUDA architecture,” said NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang in a statement. “This historic collaboration tightly couples NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing stack with Intel’s CPUs and the vast x86 ecosystem — a fusion of two world-class platforms. Together, we will expand our ecosystems and lay the foundation for the next era of computing.”

“Intel’s x86 architecture has been foundational to modern computing for decades — and we are innovating across our portfolio to enable the workloads of the future,” said Lip-Bu Tan, CEO of Intel. “Intel’s leading data center and client computing platforms, combined with our process technology, manufacturing and advanced packaging capabilities, will complement NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing leadership to enable new breakthroughs for the industry. We appreciate the confidence Jensen and the NVIDIA team have placed in us with their investment and look forward to the work ahead as we innovate for customers and grow our business.”

The two companies will hold a press conference at 1 p.m. EST to discuss the deal.

(This story has been updated with analyst commentary.)

data.title: Intellia rises after completion of enrollment in angioedema study
data.description:
• Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) shares jumped after it said that it has finished enrolling participants in its late-stage study of lonvoguran ziclumeran (lonvo-z) for hereditary angioedema.
  • The shares are up 20.6% so far today.
  • The results are anticipated in early 2026.
  • The company plans to submit a biologics license application later in 2026, aiming for a U.S. launch in early 2027.
  • The first patient was treated in the study in January 2025.

data.title: Mortgage rates edge down, refinancing applications tick up
data.description:
Mortgage rates continued to edge down, bringing about an uptick in refinancing applications, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.26% as of September 18, down from 6.35% last week but higher than 6.09% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.41%, compared to 5.50% last week and 5.15% a year ago.

"Mortgage rates decreased yet again this week, prompting many homeowners to refinance," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"In fact, the share of mortgage applications that were refinances reached nearly 60%, the highest since January 2022," added Khater.

Separately, the Federal Reserve trimmed its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday, acknowledging a softening labor market even as inflation perked up in recent months.

The Fed sees two more 25-bps cuts this year.

data.title: U.S. stocks uplifted by Intel-Nvidia deal, a day after Fed's 25 bp rate cut
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the S&P 500 (SP500) rallying, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.6% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%.

Markets were helped by strong premarket action from tech firms Intel (INTC) +28.4% and Nvidia (NVDA) +3.5% after the firms announced a blockbuster deal on Thursday to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Also, the FOMC on Wednesday announced its decision, trimming its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25% on Wednesday. Miran dissented, arguing for a 50-basis-point cut.

“The Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates during a time when stocks are at record highs and the economy is still growing, which is a very unique backdrop, as Fed cuts are typically associated with a problematic economy. This dynamic is bullish for stocks,” said Robert Schein, CIO at Blanke Schein Wealth Management.

“Now that rates have been moving lower and the Federal Reserve is looking to cut rates a few more times, we are even more bullish on big tech and financials stocks. Big tech stocks tend to outperform during lower interest rate environments, and financials may see a boost from additional M&A and mortgage activity that may come about from lower rates,” Schein added.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.12%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.58%.

Among Thursday's market movers, cybersecurity stocks were higher. CrowdStrike (CRWD) surged +10.7% after the company updated targets during its Fal.Con 2025 event.

On the economic side, initial jobless claims dropped more than expected in the past week. Also, the Philly Fed Manufacturing Index jumped in September, blowing past consensus.

The August U.S. Leading Economic Index fell more than expected in August, -0.5% MoM to 98.4.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: BofA’s Savita Subramanian: There are signs of earnings broadening beyond tech and AI
data.description:
Bank of America’s head of U.S. equity and quantitative strategy, Savita Subramanian, predicts earnings growth will extend beyond technology (XLK), (IYW), (VGT) and artificial intelligence (ARTY), (AIEQ) sectors, signaling a significant market broadening.

The strategist expressed continued bullishness on the S&P 500 (SP500) during an interview with CNBC, noting that despite previous market narrowness, data suggests a wider distribution of earnings growth is emerging.

“We’ve been bullish on the S&P 500. I think that continues, but I do see signs that it’s not just about AI and tech,” Subramanian said.

She emphasized that the combination of Federal Reserve rate cuts and accelerating profit growth creates a favorable environment for stocks, adding that “we’re actually seeing a pretty big pickup in growth across the board, not just the Magnificent 7 (MAGS), but everywhere.”

Regarding employment, Subramanian acknowledged AI’s impact on job markets but highlighted potential offsetting factors. “We are seeing pockets of softness, and AI is showing up in the numbers,” she noted, while pointing to likely tightness in manufacturing and skilled labor markets.

“We’ve got this environment where people want to make stuff in the U.S. again… You can’t use robots to build an entire building. So, I think that skilled manufacturing labor market could actually heat up.”

Subramanian identified long-term bond yields (SPLB) as a more significant factor for equity markets than short-term Fed rate decisions.

She explained that her quantitative analysis suggests stocks can withstand higher yields than many expect. “You don’t really see massive multiple compression until you get to 7%. That’s based on history.”

However, she cautioned that if yield curve steepening stems from declining demand for U.S. Treasuries rather than improved growth expectations, markets could face headwinds.

For sector-specific opportunities, Subramanian expressed preference for commodity-related investments. “The commodity plays right now look really, really interesting. Materials (XLB), energy (XLE), industrials (XLI), those are the areas that I think we could see a real margin growth, a pickup in sales, and we haven’t necessarily seen it yet,” she concluded.

This manufacturing-focused theme represents her top recommendation for investors seeking growth potential.

data.title: Aveanna upgraded at J.P. Morgan on risk-reward setup
data.description:
J.P. Morgan upgraded Aveanna Healthcare (NASDAQ:AVAH) to Neutral from Underweight and raised its Dec. 26 price target to $10 from $6 on Thursday, noting a balanced risk-reward setup for the home healthcare provider amid Medicaid funding cuts and sector-specific tailwinds.

Assuming JPM’s coverage of five Healthcare Facilities operators, analyst Benjamin Rossi argued that the sector has benefitted from positive trends for top-line demand and relatively stable operating conditions YTD.

However, the analyst cites risks to their growth trajectory in 2026 and 2027 amid reimbursement risks to federally backed payers such as Medicare and Medicaid, from which Aveanna (NASDAQ:AVAH) specifically generates ~10% and ~80% of its revenue, respectively.

While AVAH offers an opportunity to capitalize on the shift to home health and value-based care for complex and high-cost patients, Rossi considers longer-term concerns for the company amid a challenging rate backdrop and reimbursement risks.

Additionally, the analyst opined that the company’s current net leverage of ~5.2x has elevated its risk profile relative to peers despite a potential decline of 2x–3x times of leverage this year due to strong 2025 adjusted EBITDA and cash flow generation.

As for other stocks in his coverage, Rossi kept JPM’s Overweight recommendations for Concentra Holdings (CON), its top pick among outpatient names, and U.S. Physical Therapy (USPH) while reiterating their price targets at $31 and $110 per share, respectively.

InnovAge Holding Corp. (INNV) and Surgery Partners Inc. (SGRY) also retained their Underweight and Neutral ratings at J.P. Morgan, along with their price targets of $5 and $27 per share, respectively.

data.title: Netskope jumps 20% in market debut, valuing cybersecurity firm at $8.7B
data.description:
• Shares of Netskope (NASDAQ:NTSK) opened at $23 on Thursday, more than 20% higher than the initial public offering price and valued the cybersecurity firm at $8.7B.
  • Netskope sold 47.8M shares in the offering at $19 per share, raising approximately $908M. It previously planned to offer shares at a range between $15 and $17, but recently raised the range due to strong demand. 
  • The Santa Clara, Calif.-based company was founded in 2012 and develops cloud-based security software that helps businesses safeguard apps, websites, and data from cyber threats. 
  • Netskope generated $707M in annual recurring revenue as of July 31, up 33% year-over-year. Revenue increased 31% year-over-year to $328M for the six months ended July 31, 2025, the company added. It had a net retention rate of 118% and more than 85% of its annual recurring revenue is coming from customers who spent $100,000 or more, the company said in its S-1 filing.
  • Its cash flow in the first-half of fiscal 2026 was $9M, and it generated a net loss of $170M during the same time period.

data.title: Ramaco Resources rallies to all-time high as Jefferies touts Brook mine value potential
data.description:
Ramaco Resources (NASDAQ:METC) +20.4% to an all-time high $31.59 in Thursday's trading as Jefferies maintains its Buy rating and raises its stock price target to a new Wall Street-high $45 from $27 previously, highlighting the "massive value potential" from the scale-up of its Brook coal mine in Wyoming.

Ramaco (NASDAQ:METC) recently announced plans to upsize its Brook mine project from the initial planned base coal production level of 2M metric tons/year to 5M tons/year, implying a production increase of rare earths and critical minerals from 1,240 tons/year per Fluor's July preliminary economic assessment to ~3,400 tons/year.

Based on Ramaco's (METC) estimates, such an increase would increase the project's net present value from $1.6B to $5.1B, equating to $77/share, Jefferies analysts calculated, raising their stock price target to $45 but saying "this could prove conservative."
